name: Arab Castle Trail
name_original: Ruta del Castillo Árabe (Azul) — circular desde el aparcamiento Los Cerros
description: This moderate 6.6-mile (10.6 km) circular hike from Los Cerros car park explores diverse landscapes and the historic Alcalá la Vieja Fortres
region_intro: Bus station hiking trails in Parque Natural Los Cerros offer access to a diverse landscape shaped by the Henares River, featuring riverine environments, arid hills of clay and gypsum, and impressive limestone cliffs. The park encompasses pine forests and Mediterranean scrubland, providing varied terrain for outdoor activities. Elevated points within the park, such as the Ecce Homo peak, offer panoramic views of the surrounding region. This natural area serves as a significant green space, supporting a range of flora and fauna. Best bus station hiking trails in Parque Natural Los Cerros The most popular bus station hiking route is Henares River – Ruins of Alcalá la Vieja Fortress loop from Polígono Estela II, a 6.5 miles (10.5 km) trail that takes 3 hours 3 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the riverbanks and leads to historical fortress ruins. Another top favourite among local hikers is Parking Area – Los Cerros Forest Park loop from Alcalá de Henares, a moderate 5.0 miles (8.1 km) path. This trail offers views of the forest park and is typically completed in 2 hours 9 minutes. Local hikers also love the Los Cerros Forest Park – Tabla Pintora Pine Forest loop from Polígono Estela II, a 5.5 miles (8.8 km) trail leading through varied forest landscapes, often completed in about 2 hours 22 minutes. Hiking in Parque Natural Los Cerros is defined by riverine paths, arid hills, and pine forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to moderate ascents. The routes in Parque Natural Los Cerros are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from more than 230 reviews. More than 1800 hikers have used komoot to explore Parque Natural Los Cerros's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are accessible directly from bus stations in Parque Natural Los Cerros?
A: There are over 100 hiking trails in Parque Natural Los Cerros that are easily accessible from bus stations, offering a wide variety of options for all skill levels.

Q: Are the bus station trails in Parque Natural Los Cerros suitable for beginners?
A: Yes, many trails accessible from bus stations are suitable for beginners. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17508036" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino de los Tarayes – Los Cerros Forest Park loop from Polígono Estela II&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 2.8 km route with minimal elevation gain, perfect for a gentle introduction to the park's beauty. Overall, more than half of the available routes are rated as easy.

Q: What types of landscapes can I expect to see on hikes from bus stations in Parque Natural Los Cerros?
A: Hikes from bus stations in Parque Natural Los Cerros offer a diverse range of landscapes. You'll encounter lush riverine environments along the Henares River, dramatic clay and gypsum hills with impressive cliffs, and serene pine forests. Some routes also pass through areas of Mediterranean scrub and offer panoramic views from elevated points.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes starting from bus stations?
A: Yes, there are several excellent circular routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19817615" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Henares River – Ruins of Alcalá la Vieja Fortress loop from Polígono Estela II&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 10.5 km loop that combines river scenery with historical ruins. Another great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/23750920" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Cerros Forest Park loop from Parque Natural Los Cerros de Alcalá&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 5.3 km trail.

Q: Can I bring my dog on these bus-accessible trails?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome in Parque Natural Los Cerros, but it's always best to keep them on a leash to protect the local wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all hikers. Please be mindful of any specific signage regarding pet access on certain trails or protected areas within the park.

Q: Are there family-friendly hiking options from bus stations in Parque Natural Los Cerros?
A: Absolutely. Many of the easier routes are ideal for families. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17508036" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino de los Tarayes – Los Cerros Forest Park loop from Polígono Estela II&lt;/a&gt; is a short, easy walk perfect for families with young children, allowing exploration of the riverbanks and pine woods. The Ruta de los Tamarix, mentioned in regional research, is also a simple 2.5 km circular route suitable for families.

Q: What interesting landmarks or attractions can I see along bus station hiking trails?
A: Many trails lead to or pass by significant landmarks. You can explore the historical &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2393087" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruins of Alcalá la Vieja Fortress&lt;/a&gt;, which offers great views of Alcalá and the Henares river. The park also features the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2391975" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Malvecino Summit&lt;/a&gt; and the intriguing &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2391657" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gigantones Cave&lt;/a&gt;. Don't miss the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1457141" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Interpretation Center and Fountain of the Alcalá Hills&lt;/a&gt; for more insights into the park's ecology.

Q: What is the best time of year to hike in Parque Natural Los Cerros from a bus station?
A: Spring and autumn are generally the best times for hiking in Parque Natural Los Cerros. The temperatures are mild, and the natural scenery is vibrant. Spring brings wildflowers, while autumn offers beautiful foliage. Summers can be hot, so early morning or late afternoon hikes are recommended, and winters are usually mild but can be chilly.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Parque Natural Los Cerros?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars. Reviewers often praise the diverse landscapes, the tranquility of the natural environment, and the accessibility of the trails, especially those starting conveniently from bus stations. The panoramic views from elevated points are also frequently highlighted as a favorite feature.

Q: Are there any viewpoints offering panoramic vistas on bus-accessible routes?
A: Yes, several routes provide excellent viewpoints. From higher points within the 'Cerros' (hills), particularly the Ecce Homo peak (though this specific route might require a longer hike than typical bus-accessible ones), you can enjoy spectacular panoramic views that can encompass the cities of Madrid and Guadalajara, and even the Sierra de Guadarrama and Ayllón mountains. Many trails offer glimpses of the Henares River valley and the surrounding plains.

Q: Can I spot wildlife while hiking from bus stations in Parque Natural Los Cerros?
A: Parque Natural Los Cerros is rich in biodiversity, making wildlife spotting a real possibility. Keep an eye out for various bird species, including aquatic and fishing birds near the Henares River. Small mammals like foxes and rabbits are also present, and the park is home to a wide array of reptiles and insects. Early mornings or late afternoons are often the best times for wildlife observation.

Q: Are there any facilities like cafes or visitor centers near the bus station trailheads?
A: While direct cafes at every trailhead might be limited, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1457141" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Interpretation Center and Fountain of the Alcalá Hills&lt;/a&gt; serves as a valuable resource for visitors, offering information about the park. For cafes and restaurants, you would typically find more options closer to the urban areas of Alcalá de Henares, which are well-connected by bus.

It is a small hill but with steep slopes that must be climbed and descended with great care.
Easily accessible cave at the base of the Malvecino hill. The main structure is L-shaped, although there are two additional smaller corridors on the sides.

Alcalá la Vieja is the name given to the Arab fortress al-Qal'at abd al-Salam after the recovery of the territory by the Archbishop of Toledo in 1118. The term "the fortress" in Arabic (al-Qal'at ) is what today gives its name to the modern city (built around the Burgo de Santiuste, on the right bank of the river).

It is part of the set of sites that can be visited in the Community of Madrid, although it is currently fenced off and you have to settle for seeing it from the fence.

faqs.question: Where does the Ruta del Castillo Árabe (Azul) trail start, and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The trail officially starts from the Los Cerros car park in the Parque de los Cerros de Alcalá de Henares. This car park serves as the main access point for the circular route.

faqs.question: How can I reach the trailhead by public transport?
faqs.answer: While the trail is designed to start from the Los Cerros car park, public transport options to the immediate vicinity of the park entrance in Alcalá de Henares are available. From there, it's a short walk to the trailhead.

faqs.question: Is the Ruta del Castillo Árabe (Azul) trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is generally dog-friendly, allowing you to enjoy the hike with your canine companion. However, always ensure your dog is kept on a leash and that you clean up after them to preserve the natural environment for everyone.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike the Ruta del Castillo Árabe (Azul)?
faqs.answer: Spring and autumn are ideal for hiking this route, offering pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. During these seasons, the Mediterranean forest is at its most beautiful, and the weather is comfortable for outdoor activity. Summers can be hot, and winters might bring cooler, potentially muddy conditions.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for hiking in Parque Natural Los Cerros?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike the Ruta del Castillo Árabe (Azul) or to access the Parque Natural Los Cerros de Alcalá. It is freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Ruta del Castillo Árabe (Azul)?
faqs.answer: The path primarily consists of well-maintained forest tracks. While generally accessible, it features several inclines and declines, contributing to its moderate difficulty. Be aware that sections can become muddy during or after rainy periods.

faqs.question: Is the Ruta del Castillo Árabe (Azul) suitable for beginners or families with children?
faqs.answer: The trail is considered moderate. While many find it accessible, the 10.6 km distance and undulating terrain might be demanding for very young children or beginners unaccustomed to longer walks. It's a good option for families with older children who are used to hiking.

faqs.question: What historical and natural sights can I see along the Ruta del Castillo Árabe (Azul)?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter a mix of natural beauty and historical significance. Key sights include the Interpretation Center and Fountain of the Alcalá Hills near the start, the Malvecino Summit, and the intriguing Cueva de Malvecino. The highlight is undoubtedly the Ruins of Alcalá la Vieja Fortress, an ancient Arab castle offering panoramic views.

faqs.question: Are there any good viewpoints on the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route offers several picturesque viewpoints. You'll get scenic vistas of the park, including Monte de la Tortuga, Malvecino, and the Henares River. The ruins of Alcalá la Vieja Fortress also provide excellent panoramic views of Alcalá de Henares and the surrounding landscape.

faqs.question: Does the Ruta del Castillo Árabe (Azul) intersect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route shares sections with other official trails within the park. You'll find it intersecting with parts of the Ruta del Ecce-Homo, Ruta de la Puerta Verde, Ruta de los Tarayes, Camino del Castillo, and Camino de la Alvega, among others.

faqs.question: What should I consider if hiking the trail after rain?
faqs.answer: After rain, certain sections of the trail, particularly the forest tracks, can become quite muddy. It's advisable to wear appropriate waterproof footwear with good grip to ensure a more comfortable and safe experience.

waypoints.name: Interpretation Center and Fountain of the Alcalá Hills
Ruins of Alcalá la Vieja Fortress
